title: PET Cat Skeleton Definition v1
description: Defines the bone hierarchy, joint positions, rest angles, sprite mapping, and draw order for the PET cat character. The skeleton is shared between the renderer (FK) and the motion generator (bone rotation ordering).

canvas.description: Width and height of the sprite canvas in pixels.

displayScale.description: Scale factor for display (2 = 48px canvas displayed at 96px).

footAnchor.description: The foot anchor position [x, y] in canvas pixel coordinates. This is where the pet stands on surfaces.

sourceFacing.description: Default facing direction of the source sprite (-1 = facing left, 1 = facing right).

drawOrder.description: Render order of bone sprites from back to front.

eyePosition.description: Default eye center position [x, y] on the head sprite (local coordinates).

mouthPosition.description: Default mouth center position [x, y] on the head sprite (local coordinates).

parent.description: Parent bone id. null for the root bone.

joint.description: Joint position [x, y] in canvas pixel coordinates.

restAngle.description: Rest angle in radians relative to parent bone direction (0 = aligned with parent).

sprite.description: Sprite layer name referenced in the asset manifest. null for bones without their own sprite (e.g., ears that only affect head deformation).

rotation.description: Rotation limits [min, max] in radians relative to restAngle.

mode.description: model_driven: model outputs rotation. secondary: renderer applies physics. static: bone never rotates.

damping.description: Damping coefficient for secondary physics (0 = instant follow, 1 = no damping).

stiffness.description: Spring stiffness for secondary physics (0 = no return force, 1 = instant return).
